A person can hold many bookings over time.
A booking is a reservation of one person on one flight; it records the chosen seat, the fare class and the date it was made, and it is given its own booking reference.
Each flight is operated by exactly one airline and uses exactly one aircraft, and has a departure airport and an arrival airport, which are distinct operational roles.
A flight has a scheduled departure date-time and a scheduled arrival date-time.
A flight has a single current operational status from a controlled set of states (for example scheduled, delayed, cancelled, departed, arrived).
Each aircraft has a type, and many aircraft can share the same type; each aircraft also has its own passenger capacity.
Each airport has a city and a country.
Each airline has a name and a home country.
